title: Project — Discussion & Debate
topics: argument structure · for/against · conceding · discussion phrases
description: Projekt: strukturovaná diskuze a argumentace v angličtině.
notes: Structure: state position → give reasons → acknowledge counterargument → rebut
✓ A strong argument acknowledges the other side — then counters it
⚠️  Avoid: 'I think because...' | Use: 'I think... because...'
✓ End with a clear conclusion — not 'I don't know, it depends'

examples.en: I see your point, but the evidence suggests otherwise.
examples.cz: Chápu váš argument, ale důkazy naznačují opak.

examples.en: On the one hand, remote work saves commuting time. On the other, it can lead to isolation.
examples.cz: Na jedné straně práce z domova šetří čas na dojíždění. Na druhé straně může vést k izolaci.

examples.en: In conclusion, I believe the benefits clearly outweigh the drawbacks.
examples.cz: Na závěr věřím, že přínosy jasně převažují nad nevýhodami.

quiz.sentence: ___ my opinion, social media does more harm than good.
I see your point, ___ I still disagree.
___ the one hand... On the other hand...
In ___, the evidence supports stricter regulation.

tasks: 1. Write a full for/against essay on one topic (200–250 words)
2. Prepare a 2-minute spoken argument on your chosen position
3. Find a debate topic you feel strongly about and research real data
